cross_references.finding: Sprint 12 velocity drop attributed to two engineers on leave
Onboarding time at 11 days -- intervention required
Two critical bugs identified in payment module -- fix prioritised
Bugs open count is 23 with 2 critical and 1 SLA breach
Sales department over budget in EMEA and NAMER regions by ~8%
OKRs for onboarding time and product integrations are at risk

narrative.executive_summary:
In Q1 2026, the team maintained a stable velocity averaging 48 story points per sprint, with some fluctuations due to resource availability, notably a dip in Sprint 12 linked to two engineers on leave. Key objectives around reducing customer onboarding time and shipping product integrations remain at risk, despite progress such as the delivery of an onboarding improvement plan and the successful Salesforce integration. Critical bugs in the payment module were identified and prioritized for resolution, with patching completed before quarter-end. Engineering capacity constraints have led to a freeze on new projects until March, and the SSO delivery has been deferred to Q2 following a completed security review.

Budget performance was positive overall, with total spend slightly under budget by 0.9%. However, sales departments in both EMEA and NAMER regions exceeded their budgets by approximately 8%, driven by pipeline growth. Team health appears stable with no overdue action items and ongoing efforts to address onboarding challenges and retrospective learnings. Risks remain around meeting OKRs and managing critical bugs, requiring continued focus and intervention in the coming quarter.
highlights: Stable average sprint velocity of 48 points despite resource challenges
Onboarding improvement plan reduced time from 11 to 9.5 days
Critical payment module bugs identified and patched before quarter close
SSO security review completed, build phase starting in Q2
Engineering capacity locked, no new projects until March
Five of seven action items completed with two in progress and none overdue

risks.risk: Customer onboarding time reduction objective is at risk, currently at 9.5 days versus 8-day target
Product integrations OKR at risk, potentially impacting enterprise deal unlocks
Two critical bugs in payment module could affect system stability if not fully resolved
Sales departments in EMEA and NAMER are over budget by ~8%, which may pressure future spending
Sprint velocity impacted by resource availability, as seen in Sprint 12

narrative.budget_headline: Overall budget performance is positive with a 0.9% underspend; however, sales departments in EMEA and NAMER exceeded budgets by approximately 8% due to pipeline-driven spending.
